name: El Picaio from Puçol Trail
name_original: El Picaio — Trail run desde Puçol
description: This difficult 7.7-mile trail run from Puçol offers challenging terrain and panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and Sagunto Castle.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Puçol traverse a diverse landscape, transitioning from mountainous regions in the west to a flat coastal area in the east. The western parts feature the foothills of the Serra Calderona, including Monte Picayo, offering challenging trails with significant elevation. Towards the east, the terrain flattens into the coastal plain, home to Platja Puçol and the protected marshland of La Marjal dels Moros. This variety provides a range of running experiences across different natural environments. Best jogging routes around Puçol The most popular jogging route is El Picaio from Puçol Trail, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through the mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is View from El Picaio Summit – Antennas of Picaio loop from Paratge Natural Municipal de la Costera, a difficult 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This challenging route provides panoramic views from the summit. Local runners also love the Puçol Beach Promenade – Puçol Beach loop from Platja de Puçol, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through the coastal promenade, often completed in about 35 minutes. Jogging around Puçol is defined by mountainous areas, coastal plains, and marshland trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal runs to challenging mountain trails. The routes in Puçol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 5 reviews. More than 1200 runners have used komoot to explore Puçol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running terrain can I expect around Puçol?
A: Puçol offers a diverse range of running terrains. To the west, you'll find mountainous areas like the foothills of the Serra Calderona, including Monte Picayo, which provide challenging trails with significant elevation gains. Moving eastward, the terrain flattens into the coastal plain, offering easier runs along Platja Puçol. Additionally, the protected marshland of La Marjal dels Moros provides serene paths for a unique natural running experience.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Puçol?
A: Yes, Puçol has options for all fitness levels. For an easier, more leisurely run, consider the coastal promenade. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31484490" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puçol Beach Promenade – Puçol Beach loop from Platja de Puçol&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.6-mile (5.8 km) trail that offers flat terrain and sea views, perfect for beginners or a relaxed jog.

Q: Where can I find challenging trail runs with significant elevation in Puçol?
A: For more advanced runners seeking a challenge, the mountainous areas to the west of Puçol are ideal. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832501294/el-picaio-from-pucol-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Picaio from Puçol Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 7.7-mile (12.4 km) route, offer substantial elevation gain through the foothills of the Serra Calderona. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5308826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from El Picaio Summit – Antennas of Picaio loop from Paratge Natural Municipal de la Costera&lt;/a&gt;, which provides panoramic views from the summit.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Puçol?
A: Many of the running routes around Puçol are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1673444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puçol Beach Promenade – Puçol Beach loop from Puçol&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 6.4-mile (10.3 km) coastal run. In the mountainous areas, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17579306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Calderona – Serra Calderona Natural Park loop from Paratge Natural Municipal de la Costera&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 11.5-mile (18.5 km) option.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural features can I see while running in Puçol?
A: Puçol's diverse landscape offers several scenic spots. You can enjoy breathtaking vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; in the Serra Calderona. The protected marshland of La Marjal dels Moros provides unique opportunities for birdwatching and serene runs through accessible paths. Additionally, routes in the mountainous areas often offer panoramic views of the northern coast of Valencia.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks near the running trails in Puçol?
A: Yes, some running routes can bring you close to historical sites. For instance, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3954829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sagunto Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark in the vicinity, offering a glimpse into the region's rich history. While not directly on every trail, its presence adds to the historical context of the area.

Q: What do other runners think about the jogging routes in Puçol?
A: The running routes in Puçol are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from 5 reviews. Over 1200 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes ranging from challenging mountain trails to peaceful coastal and marshland paths.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Puçol area?
A: There are over 60 dedicated running routes available around Puçol, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. This includes a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails across its diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific areas for birdwatching while running in Puçol?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal dels Moros&lt;/a&gt;, a protected marshland shared with Sagunto, is designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds. It offers accessible paths perfect for serene runs while enjoying unique birdwatching opportunities.

Q: Can I find routes that combine coastal views with a good workout?
A: Yes, Puçol's coastal plain offers routes that combine beautiful sea views with a good workout. While the beach promenade routes are generally moderate, they provide ample distance for an invigorating run alongside the Mediterranean Sea, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1673444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puçol Beach Promenade – Puçol Beach loop from Puçol&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the main geographical features that define running in Puçol?
A: Running in Puçol is characterized by its transition from mountainous areas in the west, part of the Serra Calderona foothills (like Monte Picayo and Los Monasterios), to a flat coastal plain in the east, featuring Platja Puçol. Additionally, the protected marshland of La Marjal dels Moros offers a unique natural environment for runners.
tips:
Some of the access roads are in a private (gated) community.

Double-check the road you are planning to use to get in, security doesn't let everyone in.

faqs.question: How difficult is the El Picaio trail run?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult. While the specific komoot route is around 12.4 km with 335m of elevation gain, the area is known for challenging terrain. Expect varied surfaces including paths, tracks, forest firebreaks, and single-track sections. The significant elevation changes mean you should be prepared for climbs and descents, making it more suitable for experienced trail runners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse, winding through the natural landscapes of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of 'sendas' (paths), 'pistas' (tracks), and 'cortafuegos forestales' (forest firebreaks). There are also sections of single-track trails and pine woods, traversing enchanting ravines. It's a varied and engaging surface for trail running.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight on this route is the Cruz del Monte Picaio Viewpoint, offering spectacular panoramic views. From its highest points, you can see the Mediterranean Sea, Sagunto Castle, the extensive Valencian Vega, and Puçol itself.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to run this trail is generally during the cooler months, such as spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November), to avoid the intense summer heat. Winters are also pleasant, though you might encounter cooler temperatures. Always check the weather forecast before heading out, especially for rain or extreme heat.

faqs.question: Is the El Picaio trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. However, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles, including picking up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the El Picaio trail or to enter the Sierra Calderona Natural Park for recreational activities like trail running. However, always respect park regulations and signage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Puçol?
faqs.answer: The trail originates from Puçol. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the Picaio CIM Trail races, which use similar routes, often start and end at the Puçol Sports Center. This suggests that parking facilities may be available in or around the town, providing convenient access to the trail network.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Significant portions of the trail overlap with 'De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa 01)', and it also passes through sections of 'La Conquista de Valencia', 'Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana', 'Vía Verde Ojos Negros', and 'Camí de les Penyes de Guaita'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a trail run on El Picaio?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water (especially on warmer days as water sources may be limited), snacks, a fully charged phone, and sun protection. A small first-aid kit is also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Puçol?
faqs.answer: Puçol is a town near Valencia, so you will find various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options, within the town itself. These can be a good option for pre- or post-run refreshments and meals.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The komoot tour for 'El Picaio — Trail run from Puçol' is designed as a loop, starting and ending in the same general area. This allows for a continuous and varied experience without retracing your steps.
